                     Julius Shiskin, Commissioner

               Employment and Earnings is prepared by the
           Division of Industry Employment Statistics and the
           Division of Employment and Unemployment Anal-
           ysis in collaboration with the Division of Special
           Publications. The data are collected by the Bureau
           of the Census (Department of Commerce), State
           Employment Security Agencies, and State Depart-
           ments of Labor in cooperation with the Bureau of
           Labor Statistics. A brief description of the co-
           operative statistical programs of the BLS with
           these agencies is presented in the Explanatory
           Notes. The State agencies are listed on the inside
           back cover.

               Employment and Earnings may be ordered
           through the Superintendent of Documents, U.S.
           Government Printing Office, Washington, D.C.
           20402. Subscription price per year $18 domes-

                     Because Hispanics make up only about 5 percent of the        04 French                 13 Mexicano
                 labor force, data for this group are subject to a relatively     05 Polish                 14 Fuerto Rican
                 large degree of sampling error. These errors are particularly
                 large for small estimates, such as those for persons of Cuban    06 Russian                15 Cuban
                 origin. Please refer to the Explanatory Notes in this publi-     07 English                16 Central or South American

                 estimates.

                     All of the Hispanic data published by the Bureau are
                 derived from the Current Population Survey, a sample                                       21 Black
                 survey of 56,000 households conducted for the BLS by                                         OR
                 the Bureau of the Census. Identification of persons of
                                                                                                 30 Another group not listed
                 Hispanic origin is obtained from responses to the survey
                 question, "What is your origin or descent?" Respondents
                 are shown a "Flashcard," a facsimile of which appears as
                                                                                  FORM CPS-597              U.S. DEPARTMENT OF COMMERCE
                 Figure 1, and if they indicate any of the categories 10-17,      (1-3-74)         SOCIAL AND ECONOMIC STATISTICS ADMIN.
                                                                                                                    BUREAU OF THE CENSUS
                 they are identified as being of Hispanic origin. Persons who
                 report themselves as Mexican-American, Chicano, Mexican,                          ORIGIN FLASHCARD
                 or Mexicano are consolidated into the one category,                           CURRENT POPULATION SURVEY
                 Mexican origin.

                                                                     Explanatory Notes

                                 These explanatory notes provide information on the concepts, methodology, and scope of Household Data
                                 (A tables), Establishment Data (B, C, and D tables), and State and Area Unemployment Data (E table) pub-
                                 lished in Employment and Earnings.



                                                                             Introduction

                  The statistics in this periodical are compiled from two major            Employment
               sources: (1) Household interviews, and (2) reports from employers.
                                                                                           Coverage. The household survey definition of employment com-

                                                                                             The data exclude proprietors, the self-employed, unpaid volunteer

                   Under cooperative arrangements with State agencies, the re-                Persons on establishment payrolls who are on paid sick leave
               spondent fills out a single employment or labor turnover report-          (when pay is received directly from the firm), on paid holiday or
               ing form, which is then used for national, State, and area estimates.     paid vacation, or who work during a part of the pay period and are
               This eliminates duplicate reporting on the part of respondents, and       unemployed or on strike during the rest of the period, are counted
